description_long: This is a rustic wooden directional signpost featuring four arrow-shaped planks attached to a central vertical pole. The planks are arranged at different angles, each pointing in a distinct direction, and are inscribed with faded, hand-painted text. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain and some irregularities, giving it a natural, aged look. A vine with green leaves winds around the lower part of the post, adding a touch of greenery and suggesting outdoor placement. The sign is constructed primarily from wood, with the vine as a decorative element. This type of sign is commonly found in parks, trails, or outdoor recreational areas to guide pedestrians.
description: A weathered wooden directional signpost with four arrow-shaped planks and a vine wrapped around its base. The planks display faded text and point in different directions.
description_view_0: The front view shows all four arrow-shaped planks with faded text and the vine clearly visible wrapping around the post.
description_view_1: A side view reveals the thin profile of the planks and the vine, with minimal text or detail visible.
description_view_2: The back view displays the plain wooden backs of the planks and the vine partially visible, with no text present.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the thin profile of the planks and vine, similar to view 1, with no text visible.
